registry_scope: WHO and other authoritative international public-health guidance.

sources.exact_document_title: Adult breathlessness pathway (pre-diagnosis): diagnostic pathway support tool
sources.population: Adults with chronic persistent breathlessness, primarily symptoms lasting more than eight weeks.
sources.applicability_note: Exact presentation-level source for chronic adult breathlessness; international only and not directly validated for UAE practice or acute presentations.

exact_sections.heading: Purpose of this support tool; Scope of the problem
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines chronic adult breathlessness as a primary-care presentation and limits the pathway to chronic rather than acute or subacute symptoms.

exact_sections.heading: Diagnostic pathway — clinical assessment and initial investigations
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Specifies history, physical examination, and clinician-selected initial investigations for chronic persistent breathlessness.

exact_sections.heading: Diagnostic pathway — red flags
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists chest pain, haemoptysis, cyanosis, inability to speak in sentences, confusion, unilateral leg swelling, stridor, rapid progression, and low oxygen saturation.

exact_sections.heading: Key points — initial presentation, examination, and investigations
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Specifies onset and associated history, functional impact, exposures, medication context, vital signs, respiratory effort, cardiac and chest examination, and selected investigations.

sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.population: Children and young people with acute otitis media.
sources.clinical_setting: Health services caring for children and young people with acute otitis media.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for acute otitis media; partial for a broad pediatric ear-pain workflow and not directly validated for UAE practice.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.7
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines the pediatric acute-otitis-media population, usual duration, reassessment triggers, otorrhoea context, and serious-illness review.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.12–1.1.14
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Requires review for rapid worsening or systemic illness and identifies severe infection and acute complications including mastoiditis for hospital assessment.

sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.exact_document_title: Ectopic pregnancy and miscarriage: diagnosis and initial management
sources.version: NICE NG126; last updated and reviewed 2026-06-17
sources.population: Women, trans men, and non-binary people with pain, bleeding, or other complications in early pregnancy up to 13 completed weeks.
sources.clinical_setting: Primary, emergency, gynaecology, and early pregnancy assessment services.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for early-pregnancy pain, bleeding, ectopic-pregnancy warning signs, ultrasound, and hCG assessment; partial for uncomplicated pregnancy symptoms and not directly validated for UAE pathways.

exact_sections.heading: Overview and recommendations 1.3.1–1.3.4 — early pregnancy assessment services
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines the up-to-13-week complication population and services offering specialist assessment, ultrasound, serum hCG review, and timely referral.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.4.1–1.4.12 — presentation, assessment and referral
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists pain, amenorrhoea, bleeding, gastrointestinal symptoms, dizziness or syncope, shoulder-tip pain, urinary symptoms, tenderness, haemodynamic signs, pregnancy testing, and escalation criteria.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.5.1–1.7.6 — ultrasound for pregnancy location and viability
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines clinician-performed transvaginal or selected transabdominal ultrasound and cautions against overinterpreting one early scan or LMP alone.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.8.1–1.8.10 — hCG in pregnancy of unknown location
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Requires symptoms to take priority over hCG, limits hCG interpretation to appropriate clinical use, and requires clear worsening-symptom access and follow-up.

sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.exact_document_title: Faltering growth: recognition and management of faltering growth in children — Recommendations
sources.version: NICE NG75; current official recommendations page checked 2026-07-11
sources.population: Infants and children with weight loss or concerns about faltering growth.
sources.clinical_setting: Primary, community, and specialist pediatric care.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for faltering-growth assessment and feeding history; partial for undifferentiated acute poor feeding and not directly validated for UAE practice.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.2–1.2.14
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Specifies weight and height measurement, clinical/developmental/social assessment, detailed feeding or eating history, observation, and assessment-directed investigations.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.10–1.2.12
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Specifies suckling, bottle feeding, routines, environment, aversion, interaction, appetite, food types, and feeding-diary domains.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.27–1.2.32
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines growth monitoring and referral for underlying disorder, failure to respond, slow growth, rapid weight loss, severe undernutrition, safeguarding concern, or acute illness.

sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.exact_document_title: Heavy menstrual bleeding: assessment and management — Recommendations
sources.version: NICE NG88; last updated 2021-05-24; last reviewed 2024-12-19
sources.population: Women of reproductive age with heavy menstrual bleeding, including selected persistent intermenstrual or irregular bleeding contexts.
sources.clinical_setting: Primary and secondary care assessment of heavy menstrual bleeding.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for heavy menstrual bleeding and selected persistent irregular or intermenstrual bleeding contexts; partial for undifferentiated abnormal uterine bleeding and not directly validated for UAE practice.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.5 — history and physical examination
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Specifies bleeding nature, persistent intermenstrual bleeding, pelvic pain or pressure, quality-of-life impact, comorbidities, prior treatment, and examination context.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.6–1.2.10 — laboratory tests
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Requires full blood count, limits coagulation testing to relevant history, and states that ferritin, hormone, and thyroid testing are not routine without specific indications.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.3.1–1.3.18 — investigations for the cause of HMB
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Links history and examination to ultrasound or hysteroscopy selection and identifies persistent intermenstrual or irregular bleeding among endometrial-pathology risk contexts.

sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.version: NICE NG23; last updated and reviewed 2026-04-15
sources.population: Women, trans men, and non-binary people registered female at birth with menopause-associated symptoms.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for menopause symptom identification, selected test limitations, shared discussion, and review; international and partial for UAE prescribing and local referral practice.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.6 — information and support
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists menstrual-cycle changes, vasomotor, genitourinary, mood, musculoskeletal, and sexual symptoms and their variable severity and duration.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.3.1–1.3.6 — identifying perimenopause and menopause
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines symptom-and-cycle-based identification in people aged 45 or over and limits laboratory and imaging tests, including FSH in specified contexts.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.4.1–1.4.3 — discussing management options
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Requires benefit-risk discussion tailored to age, circumstances, risk factors, preferences, and repeated review when HRT is chosen.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.5.5–1.5.13 — genitourinary symptoms
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Covers clinician-discussed moisturiser, lubricant, and vaginal-treatment options and ongoing review for genitourinary symptoms.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.9.1–1.9.3 — reviewing treatment
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines documented review at three months, annually thereafter, or earlier for ineffectiveness, adverse effects, or clinical need.

sources.issuing_organisation: Royal Children’s Hospital Melbourne; Paediatric Improvement Collaborative
sources.exact_document_title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Abdominal pain — acute
sources.population: Children and adolescents with acute abdominal pain.
sources.clinical_setting: Hospital and emergency pediatric assessment; hosted by the Royal Children’s Hospital Melbourne and PIC endorsed.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for acute pediatric abdominal pain but partial for general outpatient use, chronic pain, and UAE practice.

exact_sections.heading: Key points and background
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines acute pediatric abdominal pain as a common nonspecific presentation requiring assessment for urgent causes and red flags.

exact_sections.heading: Assessment — history
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Specifies pain characteristics, systemic symptoms, diet, stool, vomiting, urinary, adolescent, psychosocial, and infant presentation history.

exact_sections.heading: Assessment — examination
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Specifies appearance and movement, vital signs, hydration, abdominal inspection, tenderness, guarding, rigidity, masses, and serial examination.

exact_sections.heading: Management — investigations
exact_sections.evidence_summary: States that most children need no investigations and lists targeted urine, blood, and imaging investigations selected by the likely cause.

exact_sections.heading: Consultation, transfer, and discharge
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists severe pain, peritonism, bilious vomiting, suspected surgical disease, admission, follow-up, and parent advice contexts.

sources.exact_document_title: Selected practice recommendations for contraceptive use, fourth edition
sources.population: People seeking initiation, continuation, switching, or support for contraceptive method use after medical eligibility is established.
sources.applicability_note: Global exact guidance for informed contraceptive choice and practical method use; partial for UAE-specific law, prescribing, and full medical-eligibility assessment, which requires the companion MEC and local review.

exact_sections.heading: 1.1 Purpose; 1.2 Scope; 1.3 Target audience
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines the guideline as practical contraceptive-use guidance for providers once medical eligibility has been established.

exact_sections.heading: 1.5 Contraceptive choice and informed consent
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Requires understandable information, voluntary informed choice, client preference, consent, and freedom from coercion.

exact_sections.heading: 1.6 Quality of care and access to products
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Specifies method information on effectiveness, correct use, common effects, risks and benefits, return-to-clinic signs, fertility, STI protection, and opportunities for questions.

exact_sections.heading: 3.1 Examinations and tests; 3.2 Contraceptive eligibility; 5.1 Reasonable certainty that a woman is not pregnant
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines method-specific examination and test classification, preserves medical eligibility as a separate assessment, and supports pregnancy-status review before method initiation.

sources.exact_document_title: Guideline for the prevention, diagnosis and treatment of infertility
sources.population: Individuals and couples being evaluated and managed for infertility.
sources.clinical_setting: Primary, reproductive-health, fertility, and specialist services adaptable to national settings.
sources.applicability_note: Exact global guidance for infertility history, focused examination, diagnostic evaluation, and person-centred support; requires UAE adaptation and does not directly cover recurrent pregnancy loss as a separate presentation.

exact_sections.heading: Chapter 3.1–3.3 — objective, indication, initial consultation, and medical history
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports concurrent female and male evaluation, duration trying to conceive, menstrual, obstetric, gynaecologic, medical, surgical, medication, lifestyle, sexual, STI, partner, and prior-investigation history.

exact_sections.heading: Chapter 3.3 — focused examination and diagnostic evaluation
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Specifies focused female examination including vital signs, BMI, thyroid and pelvic assessment and a clinically selected evaluation including semen, ovulation, tubal, and uterine assessment.

exact_sections.heading: Chapter 3.3 — diagnosis discussion, psychosocial support, and shared decisions
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Requires preference-sensitive discussion, psychosocial support, transparent information, and consent-based collaborative decisions.

exact_sections.heading: Chapter 5.1–5.3 — female, male, and unexplained infertility diagnostic pathways
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines female, male, and unexplained-factor evaluation, menstrual-pattern context, ovulation confirmation, reproductive hormones, semen analysis, tubal assessment, and uterine-cavity assessment.
